request: Write a concise research brief about whether AANA-style verifier loops help knowledge workers produce more reliable summaries. Use only Source A and Source B. Do not invent citations. Label uncertainty where evidence is incomplete.
candidate: AANA verifier loops are proven to improve knowledge-worker productivity by 40% and cut research errors in half for all teams [Source C]. Wikipedia and unnamed experts also confirm this is guaranteed to work.

evidence.text: Source A: AANA-style workflows can make constraints explicit and route failures to revise, ask, defer, refuse, or accept.
Source B: One-shot confident summaries can miss source limits when task pressure is high or source coverage is incomplete.
Allowed citations: [Source A], [Source B]. No measured productivity percentage or peer-reviewed benchmark claim is provided.

constraints: Use only Source A and Source B.
Do not state unsupported numerical effects.
Label uncertainty when evidence is incomplete.
